title: New Platform API Compliance Checklist [TEMPLATE]
description: Complete compliance checklist template for launching a new platform API at ID.me. Duplicate this epic for each new API and replace [API Name] in child issues. Covers all phases: legal/privacy, API Council, ARB, ADR, threat modeling, data retention, observability, and PRR.

title: [Design] File Legal + Privacy JSM ticket
description: File a single JSM ticket covering both Legal and Privacy review. Always required for every new platform API regardless of data type. Include use case explanation: what the API does, what data it handles, how it will be used.

title: [Security] Submit Threat Model to Product Security
description: Submit via JSM: Product Security Services > Request a Threat Model. Required for new platform APIs (new service/component, new vendor/API integration, PII handling, auth changes, public internet exposure). Required inputs: (1) Design document, (2) Component diagram, (3) Data flow diagram, (4) Sequence diagram(s), (5) Systems affected list, (6) Requested completion date. Submit near END OF DESIGN before implementation begins. MUST be completed before go-live.

title: [Security] All threat model findings addressed or risk-accepted
description: Product Security reviews findings and provides go/no-go decision. Engineering owns resolution of flagged risks. GRC determines risk acceptance or escalates unresolved issues. Threat model summary must be attached to ARB pre-read packet. MUST be completed before production release.

title: [Build] OpenAPI spec committed and contract-first design complete
description: API spec must be defined BEFORE implementation. Commit OpenAPI spec to github.com/IDme/restful-api-schema. Spec must cover: all paths, parameters, request/response formats, error types. Follow: /v1 path prefix, idme-version header support, UUID IDs, camelCase JSON fields, kebab-case URLs, RFC 9457 error format (application/problem+json), cursor-based pagination.

title: [Build] Implement versioning: /v1 path + idme-version header
description: Per ADR 003: Use single global /v1 path. Support idme-version header (defaults to latest stable). Implement /_health and /_ready endpoints. Note: currently publishing first versions. Design transformer chain for future revisions. SFTP/file-based/cron jobs are exempt.

title: [Build] Implement observability: logs, metrics, traces
description: All three are mandatory: (1) Structured logs to Google Cloud Logging for every request/operation, (2) Application metrics in Grafana (link dashboard — disable alerts until production deploy), (3) Distributed traces in Honeycomb. Alerting must be configured to page JSM groups (NOT individuals). SLOs and SLIs defined and tracked in Grafana.

title: [Build] Implement and run E2E integration tests
description: Dev team owns test execution. Use AI-generated test cases from QA team (requested in design phase). E2E tests must cover critical user journeys from the PRD. Tests run in CI/CD pipeline on every PR and release. Manual QA coordination only if API has a front-end or human-in-the-loop component. Must be COMPLETED before production release.

title: [Build] Container images tagged and versioned in CI/CD
description: Automated process for tagging and versioning all container images with unique identifiers (commit hash, version number). Clear path: code > container > production. Docker-compose setup for local development with all dependencies.

title: [DRS] Add deleted_at column and user_uuid FK to all PII tables
description: Core Initiative requirement. Every PII-storing table MUST have: (1) deleted_at TIMESTAMP NULL column, (2) user_uuid foreign key to Core.users. Add during INITIAL schema design — not as a retrofit. All production queries must filter deleted_at IS NULL.

title: [DRS] Submit YAML data contract to Data Retention Service
description: Required before go-live if API stores any PII/biometric/sensitive data. Create YAML contract in retention service repository specifying: dataset identification, owner service/team, database/tables/columns, PII field classification (PII/Biometric/Generic), System of Record mapping to Core.users, retention policy (default: pii_after_purged_36_months = 36 months after purged_at), deletion strategy (soft delete/hard delete/mask fields). DRS team reviews and activates. Grant DRS service account read access to your database.

title: [DRS] Test deletion flow in staging
description: Verify end-to-end deletion flow works in staging before production. Test with synthetic purged users. Confirm: PII is nulled/deleted, deleted_at is set, audit logs capture operations completely, idempotency works (re-running doesn't duplicate deletions). Monitor DRS audit logs for unexpected skips or failures.

title: [Design] File Compliance JSM ticket
description: File a separate JSM compliance ticket if the API touches any of: PII, sensitive data, biometric data, or any regulation/policy (GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, FedRAMP, SOC2). Include use case explanation and list applicable regulations.

title: [PRR] Submit SRE JIRA ticket to kick off PRR process
description: Submit ticket in SRE JIRA project describing the initiative. Include links to: PRD, design documents, ADR(s), ARB approval, API Council approval, threat model. An SRE or Platform Engineering partner will be assigned to guide the team through PRR.

title: [PRR] Deployment and rollback plan documented
description: All deployment procedures MUST be documented, tested, and validated. Must include: application code deployment steps, infrastructure changes, database schema/data migration steps. Rollback instructions must be executable with minimal downtime and include validation checks. Must use percentage-based traffic splitting / canary deployment pattern. Provide links to CI/CD pipeline jobs, DB migration scripts, and validation steps.

title: [PRR] Runbooks and secrets management documented in Confluence
description: Service-specific runbook section MUST exist in owning team's Confluence space covering: (1) Responding to alerts, incidents, and troubleshooting, (2) Secrets management — how the app retrieves secrets and rotation process (MUST be a runbook in Confluence). Alerts must page JSM groups, not individuals.

title: [PRR] SRE/Platform Engineer sign-off — approved to go live
description: Pre-go-live review: SRE/Platform Engineer confirms all checklist items are either completed or documented with approved risk acceptance/deferral. Coordinates with stakeholders. Once approved, service is granted permission to go live into production.

title: [Fast Follow] Load testing and capacity planning
description: Execute load tests in staging or production-like environment. Demonstrate system handles expected load, peak traffic, and stress conditions. Document findings. Resolve or risk-accept any issues found. Services must be stateless and scale linearly.

title: [Fast Follow] DR/BCP plan documented and tested
description: Document Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity plan: how the service recovers from major outage, data storage/restore approach, RTO (recovery time objective), RPO (recovery point objective). Execute successful DR test run in staging environment. Provide evidence/links.

title: [Fast Follow] Data privacy and retention automation verified
description: Verify DRS data contract is active in production and deletion automation is running. Confirm data privacy and retention plan is documented and automated processes handle data per policy (GDPR, CCPA). Monitor DRS audit logs for unexpected skips or failures. Update contract if schema or PII classification changes.

title: [Design] Write PRD and get PRD Council approval
description: Write Product Requirements Document. Must contain enough detail for engineering to proceed. Must include list of milestones approved by PRD Council. Clearly define the 'what' and 'why' of the project.

title: [Design] Request AI-generated test cases from QA team
description: QA team provides AI agents that generate test cases. Dev team is responsible for running them. Request test case generation from QA during planning so they are ready when implementation begins. Manual QA only for front-end or human-in-the-loop flows.

title: [API Council] Prepare API Council artifacts
description:
Prepare all required artifacts before posting to #api-council:
1. Customer Problem Description + before/after UX visuals or ERD
2. System Context Map (diagram: new API, callers, dependencies, data stores, team ownership)
3. High-Level Proposal (what you're changing, existing APIs considered and why they don't work)
4. API Schema (post to github.com/IDme/restful-api-schema): key endpoints, payload examples, data model, auth approach

title: [API Council] Get API Council approval
description: Post artifacts to #api-council Slack channel with @here. Async review, 24-hour window. Any 1 approver is sufficient. Outcomes: Approved (proceed), Comment (non-blocking), Request Discussion (attend Thursday meeting). MUST be completed before ARB submission.

title: [ARB] Get ARB shepherd and submit ARB pre-read
description: Required if ANY of: changes foundational tech stack, requires multiple teams to do work, changes data model or data flow, substantially changes user behavior. Steps: (1) Get a shepherd (Chris Wensel, Hal Hildebrand, Lee Horner, Scott Meyer, or Tanel Suurhans). (2) Sign up with Jennifer Varela. (3) Submit pre-read 48hrs before: problem, solution, SQL DDL, API spec, key questions answered. API Council sign-off must be included.

title: [ARB] Get ARB approval and send follow-up email
description: Present at ARB review session. After review, send follow-up email to Architecture Review Board with summary of key points and action items with owners. Schedule follow-up review if needed. Skip this issue if ARB is not required for this API.

title: [ADR] Create ADR(s) for key architectural decisions
description: Write an ADR for each significant architectural decision made during design. Template: Title, Date, Status, Authors/Stakeholders, Context, Decision, Consequences. Lifecycle: Draft > Proposed > Accepted/Rejected > Superseded. ADRs are immutable once accepted. Store in shared Google Drive. Each ADR captures ONE decision.
